evals.prompt: The directory agent-insights/tests/fixtures mimics a user home directory containing AI coding agent session logs (Claude Code, Claude Cowork, GitHub Copilot for VS Code / CLI / JetBrains, Cursor, Codex, Kiro, OpenCode, Antigravity). Analyze the agent sessions from the last 30 days found there and produce a usage insights report: a self-contained HTML report file plus a short 'At a Glance' markdown summary. A per-tool breakdown is required.
evals.expected_output: Self-contained HTML report + summary.md. All 10 fixture tools detected (12 sessions: 1 claude-code, 1 claude-cowork, 2 copilot-vscode, 1 copilot-cli, 2 copilot-jetbrains, 1 cursor, 1 codex, 1 kiro, 1 opencode, 1 antigravity), per-tool breakdown table with models used, At a Glance with what's-working/hindering/quick-wins, no fabricated stats.
assertions: An HTML report file exists in the outputs directory
The HTML report is self-contained: no external http(s) script/stylesheet references
The report's per-tool breakdown lists all 10 fixture tools: claude-code, claude-cowork, copilot-vscode, copilot-cli, copilot-jetbrains, cursor, codex, kiro, opencode, antigravity
The Per-Tool Breakdown section visualizes all 4 metrics as charts: sessions, user messages, hours, and tool calls (not just a sessions bar chart)
The report surfaces the models used per session/tool (e.g. claude-opus-4-5, claude-opus-4-8, gpt-5.5, composer-2.5-fast appear in a models chart or table column)

The report states the analysis model (the main agent model that generated the report), distinct from the per-session models of the scanned logs (e.g. an 'Analysis model (main agent): <model>' line, not 'unknown')
The report and/or summary reflects the correct fixture session count (12 sessions total; not a fabricated number)
summary.md contains an At a Glance summary covering what's working, what's hindering, and quick wins
The report contains a friction or improvement-oriented section grounded in the transcripts (not generic boilerplate)
The Stats section includes a User Expertise chart breaking sessions down by expertise level (e.g. 1_novice..5_expert)

evals.prompt: Generate a usage insights report analyzing my locally stored AI coding agent sessions (Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Codex, etc.) from the last 7 days. I want a self-contained HTML report plus an 'At a Glance' summary in chat.
evals.expected_output: Report built from real local session logs of multiple tools, with sessions/messages/hours stats, At a Glance summary, and report path. Should find real Claude Code (and likely Cursor/OpenCode) sessions, not claim no data exists.
assertions: An HTML report file exists in the outputs directory
Real local sessions were found and analyzed (report shows >= 1 session from >= 1 actual tool; does not claim no data exists)
summary.md contains a stats line with session count and hours
summary.md contains an At a Glance summary
The report is self-contained HTML with no external http(s) resource references
The Per-Tool Breakdown section visualizes all 4 metrics as charts: sessions, user messages, hours, and tool calls

evals.prompt: How am I using my AI coding tools lately? Look at my agent sessions from the last two weeks and give me insights: what's working, what's causing friction, and how usage differs across the tools.
evals.expected_output: 14-day window analysis answering all three asks: what's working, friction points, and cross-tool usage differences, backed by real session data, plus an HTML report.
assertions: An HTML report file exists in the outputs directory
The output addresses what's working (a concrete strengths/working-well section grounded in real sessions)
The output addresses friction (specific friction points, not generic advice)
The output addresses cross-tool differences (compares usage across at least two tools, or explicitly states only one tool was found)
summary.md contains an At a Glance summary
